column5: Examples
column6: Note

column5:
这款游戏酷毙了！ / This game is super cool!
他今天的造型酷毙了。 / His look today is super cool.
你的新车酷毙了。 / Your new car is so cool.
column6: Old/dedicated slang. Fun to recognize, but not the most modern everyday expression.

column3: what’s up? / what are you doing?
column4: 你 (you) 爪 (doing[dialect]) 子 (suffix) 嘛 (sentence-final)
column5:
你爪子嘛，这么晚还不睡？ / What are you doing, still up this late?
喂，你爪子嘛呢？ / Hey, what're you up to?
你爪子嘛，半天不回消息。 / What're you doing? Haven't replied for ages.
column6: Sichuan dialect flavor. Do not treat as standard Mandarin.

column5:
他是我们班的学霸。 / He's the top student in our class.
别问我，问学霸去。 / Don't ask me, go ask the brainiac.
学霸又考了第一名。 / The straight-A student got first place again.
column6: Common casual word for someone very good at studying.

column5:
哇塞，这也太漂亮了吧！ / Wow, this is so pretty!
哇塞，你怎么瘦这么多？ / Whoa, how'd you lose so much weight?
哇塞，真的假的？ / Wow, really?
column6: Casual exclamation. Slightly playful.

column5:
我媳妇儿做饭特别好吃。 / My wifey cooks really well.
今晚我得陪媳妇儿。 / I gotta hang out with the wife tonight.
他媳妇儿可厉害了。 / His wife is seriously impressive.
column6: Northern/erhua-flavored casual word. Usage depends on relationship and region.

column5:
你这个变态，别碰我！ / You pervert, don't touch me!
这题难得变态。 / This problem is insanely hard.
他笑得有点变态。 / His smile is kind of creepy.
column6: Can be insulting or serious depending on context. Use carefully.

column5:
这家店的服务杠杠的。 / This shop's service is top-notch.
他的技术杠杠的。 / His skills are awesome.
质量杠杠的，放心买。 / Quality's solid, buy with confidence.
column6: Northeastern-flavored slang.

column4: 还 (still) 凑 (gather) 合 (fit) 吧 (sentence-final)
column5:
这饭还凑合吧。 / This food's just okay.
工作还凑合吧，不算累。 / The job's passable, not too tiring.
今天天气还凑合吧。 / The weather today is so-so.
column6: Casual, slightly modest or lukewarm evaluation.

column5:
他是中医界的泰斗。 / He's a leading authority in Chinese medicine.
这位老先生是物理学泰斗。 / This old gentleman is a grandmaster of physics.
她可是书法界的泰斗。 / She's a giant in the calligraphy world.
column6: Respectful term for an eminent expert; not exactly casual “GOAT”.

column5:
我是新来的，还是个菜鸟。 / I'm new here, still a noob.
别笑我，我打游戏是菜鸟。 / Don't laugh, I'm a noob at games.
这个菜鸟连基础都不会。 / This newbie doesn't even know the basics.
column6: Casual internet-style term. Can be teasing.

column5:
这双鞋真抗造。 / These shoes are really durable.
他身体特别抗造。 / He's got a really tough constitution.
这手机挺抗造的，摔了好几次。 / This phone's tough, dropped it many times.
column6: Northern/casual flavor. Often used for people or things that can take punishment.

column5:
她是个湖南辣妹子。 / She's a feisty Hunan girl.
别惹这辣妹子。 / Don't mess with this spicy girl.
我喜欢辣妹子那种性格。 / I like that spicy-girl personality.
column6: Colloquial and culturally flavored. Context-sensitive.

column5:
他这个人很洒脱。 / He's a really carefree guy.
活得洒脱一点，别想太多。 / Live a bit more freely, don't overthink.
她说分手就分手，真洒脱。 / She broke up just like that, so unbothered.
column6: Positive personality descriptor with a stylish/literary feel.

column2: nǐ pí zi shì bú shì yǎng le
column3: are you asking for a beating?
column4: 你 (you) 皮 (skin) 子 (suffix) 是 (be) 不 (not) 痒 (itch) 了 (particle)
column5:
你皮子是不是痒了？敢顶嘴！ / You asking for a beating? Talking back?!
再闹，你皮子是不是痒了？ / Keep it up, you want a smack?
你皮子是不是痒了？我揍你啊。 / You itching for it? I'll hit you.
column6: Rude/playful threat-like phrase. Recognition first; be careful using it.

column5:
你个小屁孩懂什么？ / What do you know, you little brat?
一群小屁孩在楼下踢球。 / A bunch of brats are playing ball downstairs.
别跟小屁孩一般见识。 / Don't stoop to a kid's level.
column6: Casual insult for a child or immature person. Use carefully.

column5:
这家伙真有意思。 / This guy is really funny.
那家伙又迟到了。 / That dude is late again.
你这家伙，跑哪儿去了？ / You, where'd you run off to?
column6: Casual word for a person; tone depends on context.

column3: not spicy, not happy / can’t enjoy food without spice
column4: 无 (no) 辣 (spicy) 不 (not) 欢 (happy)
column5:
我是无辣不欢的人。 / I can't enjoy food without spice.
四川人基本都无辣不欢。 / Sichuan people basically need spice in everything.
她无辣不欢，顿顿都要辣椒。 / She needs spice every meal.
column6: Fixed colloquial expression. Good for describing someone who loves spicy food.

column5:
我说的是真的，儿豁！ / I'm telling the truth, no cap!
儿豁，我亲眼看见的。 / For real, I saw it with my own eyes.
他真的来了，儿豁！ / He really came, no joke!
column6: Sichuan/Chongqing dialect slang. Used to mean “seriously,” “for real,” or “I’m not lying.” Recognition first unless you know the context.

column3: what the hell are you looking at?
column5:
瞅啥呀，没见过啊？ / What you looking at, never seen one?
你瞅啥呀，有事吗？ / What you staring at, got a problem?
瞅啥呀，再瞅揍你。 / What you looking at? Keep it up and I'll hit you.
column6: Northern/rude confrontational phrase. Very tone-sensitive; mostly recognition first.

column5:
你太牛逼了！ / You're freaking awesome!
这操作真牛逼。 / That move was badass.
他英语说得贼牛逼。 / His English is insanely good.
column6: Very common slang, but vulgar because the literal wording is obscene. Also written 牛B or 牛. Use carefully.
